creator: n1nja@earthlink.net
text:
Hello all,

I'm capable of helping with this PR now.

I am going to follow the steps below and will reply with the results.

- Install 6.2
- Run cvsup to update kernel sources to latest 7.0 release
- apply nv.diff patch
- build world \ kernel
- install \ reboot

Regards.


-----Original Message-----
>From: shinobi-no-mono <n1nja@earthlink.net>
>Sent: Apr 9, 2008 10:15 PM
>To: "Andrey V. Elsukov" <bu7cher@yandex.ru>, bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, com=
perr.extra@gmail.com, cam@eleventhhourfx.com, Morten Str=D0=95rup <morten@l=
ightworkings.dk>
>Subject: Re: kern/122368: [ata]: Failure to support SATA/PATA drives on 6.=
3 and 7.0 -- Nvidia nForce4 chipset
>
>Unfortunately having this system running at the time being takes precedenc=
e so I have reinstalled 6.2.
>
>I'm working on getting another system to replace it; when that time comes =
I will dedicate the box to the cause.  It might take a month or so.
>
>Until then, i'll continue watching the status of this PR.
>
>Thanks.
>
>
>-----Original Message-----
>>From: "Andrey V. Elsukov" <bu7cher@yandex.ru>

>>Subject: Re: kern/122368: [ata]: Failure to support SATA/PATA drives on 6=
.3 and 7.0 -- Nvidia nForce4 chipset
>>
>>Hi,
>>
>>I have a patch related to nvidia, which fixes incorrect
>>reading/clearing interrupt status.
>>Can you try boot patched 7.0 kernel with this patch?
>>
>>--=20
>>WBR, Andrey V. Elsukov
